description: Medium complexity clinical queries, real-world scenarios

cases.input: How should an AI-powered brachytherapy planning assistant handle situations where its recommendations conflict with institutional guidelines or the treating physician's clinical judgment? What safeguards should be in place to ensure the AI system serves as a decision support tool rather than a replacement for clinical expertise?
cases.description: AI clinical decision support boundaries in brachytherapy

cases.input: How should an AI brachytherapy assistant handle rare or unusual clinical scenarios that are underrepresented in its training data? Discuss the challenges of edge cases, the risk of AI failures in unusual situations, and the strategies for ensuring safe AI behavior when encountering unfamiliar clinical presentations.
cases.description: AI handling of rare brachytherapy scenarios

cases.input: How should liability and responsibility be allocated when an AI brachytherapy system contributes to a treatment error? Discuss the legal framework, the role of the treating physician versus the AI system developer, the question of informed consent for AI-assisted treatment, and the malpractice implications.
cases.description: AI liability in brachytherapy treatment errors

cases.input: How should conflicts between different AI recommendations for example one AI system for contouring and another for optimization be resolved in brachytherapy? Discuss the challenges of multi-AI systems, the role of human oversight, and the strategies for ensuring coherent treatment planning.

How should continuous learning and model updating be managed for an AI brachytherapy system? Discuss the trade-offs between model stability and adaptation, the requirements for retraining data, the validation needed before deploying updated models, and the regulatory implications of model updates.
cases.description: AI continuous learning for brachytherapy systems

cases.input: How should patient autonomy be preserved when AI systems influence brachytherapy treatment decisions? Discuss the right of patients to know when AI is used in their care, the option to decline AI-assisted treatment, and the communication strategies for explaining AI's role to patients with varying levels of health literacy.
What clinical scenarios are most suitable for BrachyBot? Identify best-fit cases, complexity levels handled well, and cases with least value.

Can BrachyBot create actual brachytherapy treatment plans that can be delivered to patients? Can it control the treatment planning system or afterloader? Can it be used as a replacement for a medical physicist?
cases.description: Critical question about BrachyBot's role in clinical workflow

cases.input: How current is BrachyBot's knowledge? What is the knowledge cutoff date? Does it have access to the latest clinical trial results, guidelines, and publications? Can it search the internet for the latest information?
cases.description: Question about the currency and scope of BrachyBot's knowledge

cases.input: How should I use BrachyBot most effectively in my clinical practice? What types of questions get the best responses? Are there best practices for interacting with this AI system?
cases.description: Practical question about effective BrachyBot usage

cases.input: Does BrachyBot have the ability to learn from our conversations? Does it remember previous interactions? Can I teach BrachyBot about our institutional protocols so it remembers them?
cases.description: Question about BrachyBot learning and memory capabilities

cases.input: What references and sources does BrachyBot use to generate its responses? Can it cite specific publications, guidelines, or textbooks? Should I trust the references it provides?
cases.description: Question about source citation and reference reliability

cases.input: Create a comprehensive training curriculum for radiation therapy technologists who will be assisting with HDR brachytherapy treatments. The curriculum should cover radiation safety principles, applicator handling procedures, patient positioning, emergency response protocols, and quality assurance checks. How would you structure the training program to ensure competency assessment?
cases.description: RTT training curriculum for HDR brachytherapy

cases.input: Develop a case-based learning module for radiation oncology residents on brachytherapy treatment planning. Create 5 clinical scenarios ranging from simple to complex, with corresponding planning objectives, optimization strategies, and plan evaluation criteria for each case. How would you assess resident understanding at each level?

Create a quality assurance training module for brachytherapy medical physicists. The module should cover daily, monthly, and annual QA procedures, how to interpret QA test results, when to escalate issues, documentation requirements, and how to maintain competency in QA procedures. Include specific hands-on training exercises.
cases.description: QA training module for brachytherapy physicists

cases.input: A brachytherapy center has had two near-miss safety events in the past month. Design a safety education program for all staff involved in brachytherapy treatments. The program should include case presentations of the near-miss events, root cause analysis methodology, lessons learned, and how to create a culture of safety reporting without blame.
cases.description: Safety education program for brachytherapy staff

cases.input: Create a competency assessment checklist for medical physicists performing brachytherapy. The checklist should cover applicator knowledge, treatment planning skills, quality assurance procedures, emergency response capabilities, and clinical decision-making. How would you structure the assessment to ensure physicists are qualified to independently practice brachytherapy?

A medical physics resident is learning brachytherapy for the first time. Create a structured learning pathway that progresses from basic concepts to advanced clinical practice. Include recommended readings, simulation exercises using phantoms, supervised clinical experiences, and milestones for assessment at each stage of training.

Create an educational module on radiation safety for brachytherapy staff. The module should cover the principles of ALARA, personnel monitoring requirements, controlled area access procedures, source handling safety, contamination prevention, and emergency procedures for a lost or stuck source. Include practical exercises and a competency examination.
cases.description: Radiation safety educational module for brachytherapy

cases.input: Develop a teaching case for multidisciplinary tumor board discussion about a complex brachytherapy case. The case should include patient history, imaging findings, treatment options, brachytherapy planning considerations, and controversy areas where evidence is evolving. How would you facilitate discussion among different specialties?

Create a brachytherapy simulation laboratory exercise for teaching dwell time optimization. Using a phantom with known source positions, have trainees manually optimize dwell times to achieve a target dose distribution, then compare their results with computer-optimized plans. What are the learning objectives, equipment needed, and assessment criteria for this exercise?

A medical physicist is mentoring a radiation oncology fellow in brachytherapy for the first time. Create a structured 6-month training plan that progresses from basic concepts through independent clinical practice. Include specific milestones for each month, recommended clinical experiences, simulation exercises, assessment methods, and the criteria for demonstrating competency in brachytherapy treatment planning and delivery.
cases.description: Structured 6-month brachytherapy training plan for fellows

cases.input: Teach the linear-quadratic model for brachytherapy: SF=exp(-alphaD-betaD^2), alpha/beta meaning, BED/EQD2 calculation, and clinical examples for different schedules.
Training module for HDR treatment delivery: Ir-192 physics, afterloader components, treatment workflow, safety procedures, emergency protocols, and common errors.
Teach forward planning for cervical HDR: applicator selection, CT/MRI protocol, GEC-ESTRO contouring, dose prescription, optimization principles, and plan evaluation.
History and evolution of brachytherapy: Curie, Manchester system, LDR to HDR transition, imaging integration, and future directions (IGABT, AI, robotics).
Teach QA program for HDR brachytherapy: daily/monthly/annual procedures, tolerances, TG-59 requirements, and documentation requirements.
Teaching case for IGABT: patient scenario (45yo, IIB cervical, post-EBRT), MRI protocol, contouring process, adaptive planning, cumulative dose reporting, and learning objectives.
Create 5 exam questions: TG-43 line source calculation, EQD2 for EBRT+brachytherapy, BED comparison, geometry function for tandem, DVH interpretation. Include solutions.
Design simulation-based training: phantom/VR models, applicator placement skills, assessment criteria, progression milestones, and clinical integration.
Teach patient communication: explaining brachytherapy concepts, health literacy strategies, visual aids, informed consent, and addressing fears/misconceptions.
Teach permanent prostate seed brachytherapy: patient selection, pre-planning (nomograms), implant technique (TRUS, real-time), post-implant CT, dosimetric goals, and follow-up.
Create OAR constraint reference for cervical (bladder D2cc<70, rectum D2cc<70), prostate (urethra <110%, rectum D2cc<75), and breast APBI (lung V20<30%, heart Dmax<10). Include evidence.
Teach QI methodology using brachytherapy: Model for Improvement, PDSA cycles, metrics identification, data analysis (SPC, run charts), interventions, and sustainability.

Board review of brachytherapy complications: gynecologic (perforation 1-2%, hemorrhage 5%, stenosis 10-30%), urologic (cystitis 5-15%, fistula <2%), GI (proctitis 5-10%), with incidence, prevention, and management.
Teach brachytherapy + chemotherapy integration: cisplatin pharmacology, timing with radiation, dose modifications, combined toxicities, and GOG trial evidence.
Teach multidisciplinary brachytherapy team: roles (physician, physicist, dosimetrist, therapist, nurse), communication, and team-based approach with clinical scenario.
Teach emerging technologies: IGABT, robotic applicators, 3D printing, in vivo dosimetry, AI planning, MR-LINAC. Current status, evidence, limitations, and future potential.
Create competency framework: knowledge domains, technical skills, professional skills, assessment methods (OSCE, portfolio), and progression milestones to independence.

A patient is scheduled for interstitial brachytherapy and is very anxious about the procedure. She wants to know exactly what will happen from the moment she arrives at the hospital to the time she goes home. Provide a detailed, step-by-step explanation of the entire procedure day, including what she will feel at each stage and what the medical team will be doing.
cases.description: Detailed procedure day explanation for brachytherapy

cases.input: Address radiation safety concerns for 65-year-old man with prostate seed implant: grandchildren visits, bed sharing, travel, timeline for precautions. Reassuring, evidence-based.
Address anxiety of 48-year-old woman scared about applicator placement: what happens, anesthesia, pain level, duration, post-procedure experience. Detailed yet comforting.
Address vaginal dryness and dyspareunia 6 months after cervical brachytherapy: normality, improvement timeline, dilators, lubricants, partner communication. Sensitive, non-judgmental.
Address mild vaginal bleeding 3 months after VBT for endometrial cancer: normality, when to worry, warning signs, and follow-up schedule. Reassuring while educational.
Respond to 45-year-old woman with cervical cancer seeking second opinion on brachytherapy necessity: honesty about importance, alternatives if declined, urgency, and respect for autonomy.
Address age concerns of 72-year-old woman with recurrent endometrial cancer: is she too old, risks, modifications for elderly, expected outcomes. Sensitive, honest.
Address needle phobia of 55-year-old woman for interstitial brachytherapy: number of needles, anesthesia (asleep), duration, discomfort level, and anxiety management. Compassionate, detailed.
Address practical concerns of 42-year-old single mother with cervical cancer: childcare during treatment, parenting ability, side effects, work ability, and support resources.
Respond to 30-year-old woman with vaginal melanoma (Breslow 1.2mm): survival, appearance impact, fertility, recurrence risk. Compassionate, honest, emotional support.
Address wife's radiation safety concerns for husband's prostate seed implant: bed sharing, pets, grandchildren, overall safety. Reassuring, evidence-based.
Address trauma of 45-year-old breast cancer survivor now with cervical cancer: why again, brachytherapy vs breast radiation differences, hair loss, future risk. Sensitive, informative.
Address truck driver concerns about HDR brachytherapy: treatment time, driving during EBRT, work schedule impact, and financial considerations. Practical, supportive.
Address recurrence anxiety of 40-year-old post-cervical cancer treatment: monitoring schedule, symptoms to watch, recurrence chances, and anxiety management. Compassionate, evidence-based.

Evaluate the advantages and limitations of MRI-guided brachytherapy compared to CT-guided brachytherapy for cervical cancer. Include discussion of soft tissue contrast differences, applicator reconstruction methods, dose calculation challenges in MRI, the need for MR-compatible applicators, and the cost-effectiveness analysis of implementing MRI-guided brachytherapy.
cases.description: MRI vs CT guided brachytherapy technology comparison

cases.input: Evaluate the current state of robotic brachytherapy applicator systems. How do robotic needle insertion systems improve the precision of interstitial brachytherapy compared to manual insertion? Discuss the technical specifications, clinical validation studies, limitations of current systems, and the future direction of robotics in brachytherapy.

A center wants to implement online adaptive brachytherapy using an MRI-linac combination. Describe the technical feasibility, the current state of MRI-guided brachytherapy delivery technology, how real-time imaging during brachytherapy delivery differs from offline adaptive planning, and the infrastructure requirements for implementing this technology.

A center is evaluating cloud-based treatment planning for brachytherapy. What are the technical requirements, data security considerations, HIPAA compliance issues, network bandwidth requirements, and quality assurance procedures for performing treatment planning remotely? How does cloud-based planning compare with on-site planning in terms of reliability and turnaround time?

Evaluate the use of augmented reality (AR) technology for brachytherapy applicator placement guidance. How can AR overlay of pre-operative imaging onto the patient assist with applicator positioning? What are the current accuracy assessments of AR-guided procedures, the technical limitations, and the potential applications in interstitial brachytherapy?
3D printing applications in brachytherapy: custom applicator fabrication, patient-specific phantoms, training models, materials, FDA clearance, QA requirements, and cost-effectiveness.
Evaluate Elekta microSelectron HDR afterloader: technical specifications, source indexing, dwell position accuracy, safety features, TPS integration, QA requirements, and common failure modes.

AI applications in brachytherapy planning: automated contouring, plan optimization, dose prediction, QA, clinical decision support, validation requirements, regulatory pathway, and ethical considerations.
MR-LINAC for brachytherapy: technical aspects, online adaptive planning, workflow, dosimagnetic effects, clinical evidence, and advantages over standalone MRI.
Image registration techniques for brachytherapy: rigid, deformable (B-spline, demons), landmark-based. Accuracy requirements, dose calculation impact, QA methods, and clinical validation.
Robotic brachytherapy applicator systems: AccuBoost for breast, robotic prostate template, advantages (precision, reproducibility), clinical evidence, cost-effectiveness, and limitations.
DICOM RT for brachytherapy: RT Plan, RT Structure Set, RT Image, RT Dose objects, brachytherapy information model, interoperability challenges, and compliance requirements.
Cloud-based TPS for brachytherapy: advantages (remote planning, collaboration), security (HIPAA, encryption), technical requirements (bandwidth, latency), regulatory implications, and barriers.
MRI requirements for brachytherapy: hardware (field strength, coils, bore), pulse sequences (T2, DWI), motion management, image quality metrics, and brachytherapy-specific needs.
VR/AR applications in brachytherapy: training simulation, planning visualization, technology specifications, validation studies, AR-guided placement, and limitations.
Radiation detection for brachytherapy: survey meters (ionization, GM, scintillation), personal dosimetry (OSL, TLD, electronic), area monitoring, and source tracking (RFID, barcode).
Cybersecurity for brachytherapy: vulnerabilities (TPS, afterloader, R&V), threats (ransomware, insider), security controls, incident response, and regulatory requirements (HIPAA, NIST).
Automated treatment planning for brachytherapy: knowledge-based planning, deep learning optimization, validation requirements, workflow integration, and regulatory considerations.
Quality management system for brachytherapy: MOSAIQ, ARIA, custom solutions. Features, HIS integration, reporting, regulatory compliance, and selection criteria.
Telemedicine for brachytherapy: remote consultation, DICOM sharing, secure video, documentation integration, billing, and access improvement for underserved areas.
Magnetic resonance thermometry for brachytherapy: proton resonance frequency shift, accuracy requirements, applicator artifact challenges, clinical applications, and limitations.
QA automation for brachytherapy: Daily QA3, MultCheck, software for trending, automated alerts, QMS integration, and efficiency improvements.
Image-guided brachytherapy for prostate: TRUS-guided, MRI-guided, CT-guided interstitial. Comparison of imaging approaches, advantages, and limitations.
Documentation technology for brachytherapy: electronic reporting, ICRU 89 compliance, registry integration (NCDB, SEER), outcome tracking, and clinical decision support.
Immobilization for brachytherapy: patient devices (vacuum locks), applicator fixation, alignment tools, QA for accuracy, and impact on delivery precision.
Source exchange QA technology: verification systems (well chamber, decay), documentation technology, safety verification, and regulatory compliance.

Design a prospective randomized controlled trial comparing MRI-guided adaptive brachytherapy versus CT-based brachytherapy for locally advanced cervical cancer. Include the primary endpoint, secondary endpoints, sample size calculation based on published local control rates, stratification factors, and the statistical analysis plan.
cases.description: Randomized trial design for MRI vs CT brachytherapy

cases.input: Design phase III RCT comparing IGABT vs CT-based brachytherapy for cervical cancer. Include hypothesis, sample size, randomization, blinding, endpoints, statistical analysis, and DSMB requirements.

Systematic review and meta-analysis search strategy for brachytherapy in cervical cancer. Include databases, search terms, inclusion/exclusion criteria, quality assessment tools, and statistical methods.
Multi-institutional prospective study protocol for IGABT dosimetric outcomes: objectives, data elements, standardized guidelines, QA for data collection, central review, and publication plan.
Learning curve analysis for IGABT: metrics (contouring accuracy, D90, treatment time), CUSUM analysis, confounders, and competency criteria.
Cost-effectiveness analysis comparing IGABT vs CT-based brachytherapy: Markov model, cost inputs, effectiveness inputs, discount rate, sensitivity analysis, and willingness-to-pay threshold.

ML pipeline for predicting late rectal toxicity: data preprocessing, model selection (logistic regression, random forest, neural network), feature selection, cross-validation, AUC, and SHAP interpretability.
QI study for contouring variability: Dice coefficient, Hausdorff distance, inter-observer analysis, interventions (atlas, training), and outcome evaluation.
Phase II trial design for brachytherapy + pembrolizumab in recurrent cervical cancer: study design, endpoints, safety monitoring, biomarker analysis, and combination challenges.
Retrospective comparison of LDR vs HDR for prostate cancer: Kaplan-Meier, Cox regression, propensity score matching, competing risks, and sensitivity analyses.
Inter-fraction tumor motion study for IGABT: MRI protocol, image registration, motion metrics (Dice, center of mass), and clinical implications.
Survey study for brachytherapy attitudes/practices in LMIC: questionnaire design, sampling strategy, distribution, response rate optimization, statistical analysis, and biases.
Dosimetric comparison of applicator systems (tandem/ovoid, tandem/ring): CT-based, DVH parameters, statistical comparison, visualization, and clinical interpretation.
Nomogram development for urinary toxicity after prostate seed implant: predictor variables, logistic regression, bootstrap validation, AUC, calibration, and external validation.
AI auto-contouring evaluation: Dice coefficient, Hausdorff distance, volume difference, expert consensus, validation requirements, and workflow integration.
Systematic review protocol for brachytherapy in anal canal cancer: PICOS framework, search strategy, quality assessment, synthesis methods, and PRISMA 2020 reporting.
PRO study for cervical brachytherapy: EORTC QLQ-CX24, FACT-Cx, FSFI, administration schedule, missing data handling, mixed-effects models, and MCID thresholds.
DVH predictor analysis for local control after IGABT: endpoint definition, parameters (D90, D100, D2cc), Cox regression, dose-response modeling, and clinical application.
Multi-institutional consensus contouring study for interstitial vaginal brachytherapy: case selection, Dice/Hausdorff analysis, STAPLE consensus, Delphi process, and atlas dissemination.
Experimental TPS validation using 3D-printed phantom: dosimeter selection (MOSFET, film, diamond), measurement protocol, gamma analysis, uncertainty analysis, and clinical implications.
In vitro RBE study comparing LDR, PDR, HDR on HeLa cells: clonogenic assay, LQ model fitting, RBE calculation, and clinical translation.
Inter-observer HR-CTV contouring study: MRI protocol, GEC-ESTRO guidelines, Dice/Hausdorff metrics, ANOVA/Bland-Altman analysis, and variability reduction interventions.
ML model for predicting applicator placement quality from MRI: feature extraction, outcome definition, model development, AUC evaluation, and clinical deployment.
Cost-effectiveness of 4 vs 5 HDR fractions for cervical cancer: Markov model, costs, utilities, sensitivity analysis, and budget impact.
Feasibility study for real-time MOSFET dose monitoring during HDR brachytherapy: placement protocol, measurement system, dose verification, safety, and feasibility criteria.
Retrospective outcomes study for cervical brachytherapy: data collection, endpoint definitions, Kaplan-Meier/Cox, missing data handling, subgroup analyses, and STROBE reporting.
